ask_next.question: How large is the burn — roughly what area does it cover?
ask_next.purpose: Estimate body surface area to assess severity
ask_next.why: Burns >10% body surface area or >3 inches require emergency care

response_guides.note: Large burn area — call 911, risk of fluid loss, hypothermia, and infection is severe

ask_next.question: Is the burned area on the face, hands, feet, genitals, or over a major joint?
ask_next.why: These locations need immediate medical evaluation regardless of burn depth

response_guides.note: High-risk burn location — physician evaluation required regardless of size or depth

ask_next.question: Does the burned skin look white, brown, or charred — or does it feel numb with no pain?
ask_next.why: Absence of pain with white/charred appearance = nerve destruction = 3rd degree

response_guides.note: 3rd degree burn suspected — call 911, do not attempt home treatment
